whatIs.title: # What is this?
whatIs.subtitlePrefix: If you are still confused about

card1.title: # Hosting Dream
card1.line1: Spin up containers, game servers, web apps. Configure DNS, 2FA, scoped API keys.

card2.title: # That Knows You
card2.line1: We know what our customers want and we provide that.

features.title: # Features
features.item1Text: A minimal, fast panel built for smooth workflows and instant actions.
features.item2Text: Roles, permissions, teams, and scoped API keys that work out of the box.
features.item3Text: Integrated DNS management with direct UI controls and automation.
features.item4Text: Threat detection and full audit history in one place.
features.item5Text: Connect your own nodes or manage dedicated ones through a central API.

community.title: # Community
community.subtitle: Join the network
community.description: From indie studios to enterprise infrastructure - EclipseSystems powers production workloads everywhere.

about.title: # About
about.line1: EclipseSystems was created with a simple belief: hosting should feel effortless.
about.line2: Our platform merges orchestration, security, and team operations into a single, elegant control plane. From game servers to enterprise workloads, Eclipse gives you the power to deploy, scale, and secure — without friction.

plans.title: # Portal Plans
plans.subtitle: Choose the portal that fits your workflow. All portals include access to the dashboard.

faq.title: # FAQs
faq.a1: Sign up for a free portal account and deploy your first server in minutes. Our panel guides you through every step.
faq.q2: Can I connect my own nodes?
faq.a2: YES! Eclipse supports connecting your own infrastructure. Enterprise plans include full node management.
faq.a3: Basic support includes email & community. Premium support includes priority response, dedicated channels, and direct engineering access.
faq.a4Prefix: The core panel is open-source for non-commercial purposes. Check our

cta.title: # Ready to deploy?
cta.subtitle: Join thousands of teams using EclipseSystems to power their infrastructure.

license.translationNote: This is original legally binding version of the license.

quick.title: # Quick Reference
quick.attributionPrefix: Any deployment must include visible attribution to
quick.attributionSuffix: with a link to the official repository. This applies to both frontend and backend interfaces.

definitions.title: 1. Definitions
definitions.body:
"Software" means the source code, documentation, and other materials
provided in this repository.

"User" means any individual or organization accessing the Software.

"Non-Commercial Purpose" means any use that is not intended for or
directed toward commercial advantage, monetary compensation, or
revenue-generating activity.

"Commercial Purpose" means any use of the Software in a manner that
generates revenue, provides paid services, is used in a business
context, or is deployed in production environments.

"Authorized Stewards" means EclipseSystems under Misiu LLC and
individuals explicitly designated by Maksym Huzun.

grant.title: 2. Grant of Rights

nonCommercial.title: 3. Non-Commercial Use
nonCommercial.body:
Non-profit organizations, educational institutions, researchers, and
individuals may use, modify, and run the Software for Non-Commercial
Purposes without restriction.

commercialReserved.title: 4. Commercial Use Reserved
commercialReserved.body:
All rights to use, modify, host, deploy, sublicense, integrate, or
distribute the Software for Commercial Purposes are exclusively
reserved to:

  - EclipseSystems (a project under Misiu LLC), and
  - Maksym Huzun (noname@ecli.app)

No other User is granted any commercial rights under any circumstances.

redistribution.title: 5. Redistribution
redistribution.body:
Users may redistribute unmodified copies of the Software for
Non-Commercial Purposes only, provided that:

  (a) this license is included in full, and
  (b) no fee is charged beyond reasonable distribution costs.

Redistribution for Commercial Purposes is prohibited.

derivative.title: 6. Derivative Works
derivative.body:
Users may create, modify, and share Derivative Works for
Non-Commercial Purposes only. Any Derivative Work must:

  (a) include this license in full,
  (b) clearly state that it is a derivative of the original Software,
  (c) preserve all attribution notices required by Section 9.

hosting.title: 7. Hosting and Deployment Restrictions
hosting.body:
Users may not deploy, host, or make the Software (or any Derivative
Work) publicly accessible as a service, cloud offering, or production
system, except for Non-Commercial educational, research, or testing
demonstrations.

All commercial hosting and SaaS rights are exclusively reserved to
EclipseSystems and Maksym Huzun.

preservation.title: 8. License Preservation
preservation.body:
This license and all copyright notices must be preserved in all copies,
forks, or Derivative Works, except where explicitly exempted for
Authorized Stewards.

attribution.title: 9. Attribution Requirement
attribution.body:
Any User deploying, running, or distributing the Software or any
Non-Commercial Derivative Work must preserve and display a visible
copyright and license notice that:

  (a) identifies EclipseSystems and/or Maksym Huzun as the copyright
      holders,
  (b) includes a reference or hyperlink directing back to the official
      EclipseSystems project repository, and
  (c) is reasonably accessible to end users in either the frontend
      user interface, backend administrative interface, or both.

This attribution requirement does not apply to EclipseSystems under
Misiu LLC or to Authorized Stewards designated by Maksym Huzun.

termination.title: 10. Termination
termination.body:
Any violation of this license immediately and permanently terminates
all rights granted herein. Upon termination, the User must cease all
use and destroy all copies of the Software.

noWarranty.title: 11. No Warranty
noWarranty.body:
The Software is provided "as is," without warranty of any kind,
express or implied. In no event shall the copyright holders or
Authorized Stewards be liable for any claim, damages, or other
liability arising from the use of the Software.

faq.title: # License FAQ
faq.q1: Can I use this for my school project?
faq.a1: Yes! Educational and academic use is fully permitted under this license.
faq.q2: Can I modify the code for personal learning?
faq.a2: Absolutely. You can view, modify, and experiment with the code for non-commercial purposes.
faq.q3: Can I host this as a paid service?
faq.a3: No. Commercial hosting, SaaS deployment, and revenue-generating services are reserved exclusively to EclipseSystems and Maksym Huzun.
faq.a4: We welcome contributions! Please check our contributing guidelines on GitHub for details on how to contribute.
faq.q5: Can I use this for my startup/business?
faq.a5: Not without explicit permission. All commercial use rights are reserved. Contact noname@ecli.app for commercial licensing inquiries.
faq.q6: What if I violate the license?
faq.a6: Any violation immediately terminates your license. You must cease all use and destroy all copies of the software.

contact.title: # Questions?
contact.heading: Need Commercial Access?
contact.body: Interested in using EcliPanel or its assets for your business? We offer commercial licensing and enterprise support. Get in touch to discuss your needs and how we can help you leverage EcliPanel for your commercial projects.

firewall.defaultSshPortHint: This port is reserved for SSH and cannot be updated using VM_PORTS.
firewall.noVmPortsDefined: The VM_PORTS variable is not defined for this server. Add it in Startup → Environment Variables to use this tab.
firewall.extraPortsDescription: Each entry becomes an additional host-to-guest forward via VM_PORTS.
firewall.portRuleHint: Valid entries: port, host:guest, port/tcp, port/udp. Do not use the default SSH port or guest port 22.
firewall.hostBindHelp: Optional host address for forwards, e.g. 0.0.0.0.
firewall.loadFailed: Unable to load firewall settings. Please try again later.
firewall.portMustBeValid: Port must be a valid number between 1 and 65535.
firewall.noRulesDescription: Add a port forwarding rule to expose a service from the VM.
firewall.vmPortDesc: The port your service listens on inside the VM (internal).
firewall.globalDesc: The port exposed on the host / internet (external).
firewall.hostBindNote: Host bind address is optional and applies to all forwarded ports.
firewall.invalidPortEntries: One or more port entries are invalid.
firewall.invalidPorts: Fix invalid port entries before saving.
firewall.sshPortConflict: A rule conflicts with the default SSH port; remove it before saving.

states.serverNotFoundDescription: The server you're looking for doesn't exist or is unavailable.
states.accessRevokedDescription: Your access to this server has been removed. Reloading will update your permissions.

toasts.powerDenied: Power action denied by dice roll.

alerts.transferInitiated: Transfer initiated. This may take several minutes.

confirm.deleteServer: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this server? This action cannot be undone.

power.confirmMessage: Are you sure you want to {action} the server?
power.killWarning: Killing a server forcibly terminates its process. Data loss may occur.

transfer.title: Transfer Server
transfer.description: Select the destination node to transfer this server to.
transfer.notice: This may take several minutes. Both nodes must be online.

kvm.bannerDescription: This server runs as a full virtual machine. File management and console may behave differently than container-based servers.
kvm.filesNotice: KVM filesystem is managed by cloud-init. Files shown here may not reflect the guest VM's actual filesystem. Use SSH/SFTP to access the VM directly.
kvm.killWarning: Killing a KVM virtual machine is equivalent to pulling the power plug. The guest OS will not shut down gracefully.
kvm.transferNotice: Transferring a KVM server requires both nodes to support KVM passthrough. The VM disk image will be migrated.

startup.selectDockerBeforeSave: Please select a Docker image before saving.
startup.noAlternateDocker: No alternate docker images are configured for this egg.
startup.detectionDescription: Patterns matched against console output to detect startup completion.

mounts.title: Mounts
mounts.kvmNotice: KVM filesystem is managed by cloud-init and may not display correctly in this panel.
mounts.description: Mounts bind host directories into your server container.
mounts.empty: No mounts configured. Mounts are managed by administrators.

databases.title: Databases
databases.confirmDelete: Delete this database? This will permanently DROP the database.

backups.title: Backups
backups.empty: No backups found. Create one to get started.
backups.confirmRestore: Restore this backup? Current data may be overwritten.

alerts.demoDeleteDisabled: Deleting nodes is disabled in demo mode.

form.name: Name
form.nodeIdHint: Unique identifier used by Wings and API routes.
form.fqdnHint: Optional hostname used for display and network routing.
form.backendWingsUrlHint: Internal backend-to-node URL. Use this when public and private endpoints differ.
form.sslHint: Disable if the Wings node uses HTTP. WebSockets will be proxied through the backend.
form.allowedOriginHint: The panel origin to send when proxying WebSockets to this node. Must match an entry in the Wings daemon config. Defaults to FRONTEND_URL.
form.sftpProxyPortHint: Backend TCP proxy port for clients that can't reach the node directly.

aiChatPage.initialMessage: Hey there! 👋 I'm your Eclipse AI assistant. I can help you manage servers, debug issues, and optimize your infrastructure. What would you like to work on today?

errors.noModelAvailable: No AI model available. Please contact your administrator to configure one.

sections.aiToolsDescription: Specialized AI tools for your workflow

codeAssistant.name: Code Assistant
codeAssistant.description: Generate, debug, and optimize server configurations
codeAssistant.systemPrompt: You are a Code Assistant for game server management. Help the user generate, debug, and optimize server configuration files (server.properties, spigot.yml, paper.yml, startup scripts, Docker compose files, etc). Provide clear code with explanations.
codeAssistant.placeholder: Describe what you need — e.g. 'Optimize my paper.yml for 50 players'

logAnalyzer.name: Log Analyzer
logAnalyzer.description: Analyze server logs and identify issues automatically
logAnalyzer.systemPrompt: You are a Log Analyzer for game servers. The user will paste server logs. Identify errors, warnings, performance issues, plugin conflicts, and crashes. Provide clear diagnoses and actionable fix steps.
logAnalyzer.placeholder: Paste your server log or describe the error…

imageGenerator.name: Image Generator
imageGenerator.description: Generate server icons and banners with AI

configWizard.name: Config Wizard
configWizard.systemPrompt: You are a Config Wizard for game servers. Based on the user's requirements (player count, server type, mods/plugins, hardware specs), generate complete, optimized configuration files. Output ready-to-use config blocks.
configWizard.placeholder: e.g. 'Generate optimized JVM flags for 8GB RAM, Paper 1.21, 100 players'

performanceAdvisor.name: Performance Advisor
performanceAdvisor.systemPrompt: You are a Performance Advisor for game servers. Analyze the user's described setup and provide specific optimization suggestions for TPS, memory usage, chunk loading, entity management, and network performance. Be specific with config values.
performanceAdvisor.placeholder: Describe your server setup and performance issues…

analyticsAi.name: Analytics AI
analyticsAi.description: Predictive analytics for server resource planning
analyticsAi.systemPrompt: You are an Analytics AI for server resource planning. Help the user predict resource needs, plan scaling, estimate costs, and analyze usage patterns. Provide data-driven recommendations for server sizing and capacity planning.
analyticsAi.placeholder: e.g. 'I expect 200 concurrent players, what resources do I need?'

chat.startUsing: Send a message to start using {tool}

models.myModelsDescription: Models your administrator has granted you access to
models.noModelsAssignedDescription: Contact your administrator to get access to AI models.
models.allModelsDescription: Full catalogue of available AI models

header.title: Billing
header.description: Manage your subscription and payment methods

demo.title: Demo Mode
demo.description: Demo mode simulates temporary enterprise access with limited resources and provides limited accessibility to AI models such as openai/gpt-oss-20b which can be used in AI Studio, AI Chat or for AI assistant (inline suggestions) features. Intended for testing and evaluation purposes before purchasing enterprise plan.
demo.expired: Your demo has expired. You can no longer start a new demo.
demo.used: Demo mode has already been used for this account. Contact support if you believe this is a mistake.
demo.footnote: Demo mode is a temporary sandbox. Changes made during demo may not persist after it ends, and infrastructure actions are limited.

toast.endedDescription: Your demo session has ended and your account is restored.

stats.priceFromOrder: Price from order or contact sales
